last_excerpt: but was sometimes useful. The piano stops. Either they got it or they walked away. From here there's no way to tell which, and the silence after both would sound the same. A lawnmower somewhere farther off. Friday evening mowing — that's someone who works a weekday schedule and does yard work in the last good light. The grass probably doesn't need it yet this early in spring but the mower's been sitting in the garage all winter and there's something about pulling it out for the first time. The first mow isn't really about the grass. My uncle used to mow in diagonal stripes. He was particular about it. The second pass had to cross the first at exactly the same angle mirrored. From the street it looked like argyle. Argyle is a pattern that peaked and never fully went away. It just receded to socks and golf courses.
